revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=北野天神社| 画像=| 所在地=愛知県江南市北野町天神8| 祭神=[[菅原道真]]| 社格=| 創建=[[1654年]]（[[承応]]3年）| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事=| }}
'''北野天神社'''（きたのてんじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[江南市]]にある[[神社]]である。
毎年1月中旬に行われる「筆まつり」は、字の上達を祈願しての参拝者の他、合格祈願の受験生も多く訪れる。
学業成就、合格祈願、家内安全にご利益があるという。普段は無人であるが、1月～2月の日曜日と毎月25日の月次祭には合格祈願などのご祈祷が行われる。
== 祭神 == *菅原道真
== 筆まつり == *毎年1月中旬に行われる祭りである。[[1964年]]（昭和39年）より開催されている。 *この祭りのときに境内にある石造りの大筆に願いを書くと、字が上達するといわれている。また、受験シーズンに重なることもあり、受験生も多く訪れる。 *筆まつりで行われる「大筆奉納行列」は、長さ約4m、重さ約50kgの大筆を、[[厄年]]男性と新入学児が御所車に乗せて神社まで曳き廻すものである。 *当初は菅原道真の命日（[[2月25日]]）に近い日曜日に開催されていたが、受験シーズンにあわせて、1月中旬の土・日曜日に変更されている。
== 所在地 == *愛知県江南市北野町天神8
== 交通機関 == *[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]][[江南駅 (愛知県)|江南駅]]より徒歩3分
[[Category:愛知県の神社|きたのてんしんしや]] [[Category:江南市|きたのてんしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=北野天神社| 画像=| 所在地=愛知県江南市北野町天神8| 祭神=[[菅原道真]]| 社格=| 創建=[[1654年]]（[[承応]]3年）| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事=| }}
'''北野天神社'''（きたのてんじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[江南市]]にある[[神社]]である。
毎年1月中旬に行われる「筆まつり」は、字の上達を祈願しての参拝者の他、合格祈願の受験生も多く訪れる。
学業成就、合格祈願、家内安全にご利益があるという。普段は無人であるが、1月～2月の日曜日と毎月25日の月次祭には合格祈願などのご祈祷が行われる。
== 祭神 == *菅原道真
== 筆まつり == *毎年1月中旬に行われる祭りである。[[1964年]]（昭和39年）より開催されている。 *この祭りのときに境内にある石造りの大筆に願いを書くと、字が上達するといわれている。また、受験シーズンに重なることもあり、受験生も多く訪れる。 *筆まつりで行われる「大筆奉納行列」は、長さ約4m、重さ約50kgの大筆を、[[厄年]]男性と新入学児が御所車に乗せて神社まで曳き廻すものである。 *当初は菅原道真の命日（[[2月25日]]）に近い日曜日に開催されていたが、受験シーズンにあわせて、1月中旬の土・日曜日に変更されている。
== 所在地 == *愛知県江南市北野町天神8
== 交通機関 == *[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]][[江南駅 (愛知県)|江南駅]]より徒歩3分

'''北野天神社'''（きたのてんじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[江南市]]にある[[神社]]である。
毎年1月中旬に行われる「筆まつり」は、字の上達を祈願しての参拝者の他、合格祈願の受験生も多く訪れる。
学業成就、合格祈願、家内安全にご利益があるという。普段は無人であるが、1月,2月の日曜日と毎月25日の月次祭には合格祈願などのご祈祷が行われる。
== 祭神 == * 菅原道真
== 筆まつり == * 毎年1月中旬に行われる祭りである。[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）より開催されている。 * この祭りのときに境内にある石造りの大筆に願いを書くと、字が上達するといわれている。また、受験シーズンに重なることもあり、受験生も多く訪れる。 * 筆まつりで行われる「大筆奉納行列」は、長さ約4m、重さ約50kgの大筆を、[[厄年]]男性と新入学児が御所車に乗せて神社まで曳き廻すものである。 * 当初は菅原道真の命日（[[2月25日]]）に近い日曜日に開催されていたが、受験シーズンにあわせて、1月中旬の土・日曜日に変更されている。
== 交通機関 == * [[名鉄犬山線]] [[江南駅 (愛知県)|江南駅]]より徒歩3分
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたのてんしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:江南市の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]]

'''北野天神社'''（きたのてんじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[江南市]]にある[[神社]]である。
毎年1月中旬に行われる「筆まつり」は、字の上達を祈願しての参拝者の他、大学や高校（当神社近隣には県立江南高校が所在）などの合格祈願の受験生も多く訪れる。
学業成就、合格祈願、家内安全にご利益があるという。普段は無人であるが、1月,2月の日曜日と毎月25日の月次祭には合格祈願などのご祈祷が行われる。
== 祭神 == * 菅原道真
== 筆まつり == * 毎年1月中旬に行われる祭りである。[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）より開催されている。 * この祭りのときに境内にある石造りの大筆に願いを書くと、字が上達するといわれている。また、受験シーズンに重なることもあり、受験生も多く訪れる。 * 筆まつりで行われる「大筆奉納行列」は、長さ約4m、重さ約50kgの大筆を、[[厄年]]男性と新入学児が御所車に乗せて神社まで曳き廻すものである。 * 当初は菅原道真の命日（[[2月25日]]）に近い日曜日に開催されていたが、大学受験シーズンにあわせて、1月中旬の土・日曜日に変更されている。
== 交通機関 == * [[名鉄犬山線]] [[江南駅 (愛知県)|江南駅]]より徒歩3分
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたのてんしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:江南市の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:江南市の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2015年1月11日 (日) 13:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 北野天神社 | 画像 = [[File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg|300px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[江南市]]北野町天神8 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 27.4 | 祭神 = [[菅原道真]] | 神体 = | 社格 = | 創建 = 1654年（[[承応]]3年） | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 筆まつり（1月中旬の土日） | 神事 = }} '''北野天神社'''（きたのてんじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[江南市]]北野町天神8にある[[神社]]である。
学業成就、合格祈願、家内安全にご利益があるとされる。無住の神社であるが、1月・2月の日曜日と毎月25日の月次祭には合格祈願などの祈祷が行われる。毎年1月中旬には「筆まつり」が行われ、字の上達を祈願する参拝者や学校合格を祈願する受験生などが訪れる。
== 祭神 == * [[菅原道真]]
== 歴史 == 承応3年（1654年）に創建された<ref name="江南市観光協会">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 北野天神社] 江南市観光協会</ref>。
== 祭礼 == [[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|180px|筆まつりの大筆奉納行列]]
=== 筆まつり === 毎年1月中旬には「筆まつり」が行われる。1964年（[[昭和]]39年）より開催されている。この祭りのときに境内にある石造りの大筆に願いを書くと、字が上達するといわれている。当初は菅原道真の命日（2月25日）に近い日曜日に開催されていたが、大学受験シーズンにあわせて、1月中旬の土・日曜日に変更されている。受験シーズンに重なることもあり、受験生も多く訪れる。
筆まつりで行われる「大筆奉納行列」は、長さ約4m、重さ約50kgの大筆を、[[厄年]]男性と新入学児が御所車に乗せて神社まで曳き廻すものである。
== 交通アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]][[江南駅 (愛知県)|江南駅]]より徒歩3分<ref name="江南市観光協会"/>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 北野天神社] 江南市観光協会
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたのてんしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:江南市の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:江南市の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2015年1月11日 (日) 13:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 北野天神社 | 画像 = [[File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[江南市]]北野町天神8 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 27.4 | 祭神 = [[菅原道真]] | 神体 = | 社格 = | 創建 = [[1654年]]（[[承応]]3年） | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 筆まつり（1月中旬の土日） | 神事 = }}

== Tohoku Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tome)|ja|天神社 (登米市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}, Miyagi Prefecture.
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiroi)|ja|天神社 (白井市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Isumi) - Located in Isumi, Chiba Prefecture, in Misaki Town, Choja. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nishitokyo)|ja|天神社 (西東京市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo|ja|西東京市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mitaka)|ja|天神社 (三鷹市)}} - Located in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiki)|ja|天神社 (志木市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kamisato)|ja|天神社 (上里町五明)}} - Located in Kamisato, Saitama. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Chuo)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市中央区)}} - Located in Saitama City, Chuo Ward. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Minamiku-Futonoya)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市見沼区風渡野)}} - Located in Futonoya, Minamiku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Midoriku-Terayama)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市緑区寺山)}} - Located in Terayama, Midoriku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yashio-Ozone)|ja|天神社 (八潮市大曽根)}} - Located in Ozone, Yashio City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kuki-Kitahiroshima)|ja|天神社 (久喜市北広島)}} - Located in Kitahiroshima, Kuki City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Hanyu-Kamikawasaki)|ja|天神社 (羽生市上川崎)}} - Located in Kamikawasaki, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)|ja|天神社 (加須市平永)}} - Located in Hiranaga, Kazo City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yokosuka)|ja|天神社 (横須賀市)}} ({{ill|Kurihama Tenjinja|ja|久里浜天神社}}) - Located in {{ill|Yokosuka|ja|横須賀市}}, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kurobe)|ja|天神社 (黒部市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|ja|黒部市}}, Toyama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Fujikawaguchiko-Oarashi)|ja|天神社 (富士河口湖町大嵐)}} - Located in Oarashi, {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko|ja|富士河口湖町}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Oarashi Tenjinja|ja|大嵐天神社}} * {{ill|Tenmantenjinja (Iida)|ja|天満天神社 (飯田市)}} - Located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, Nagano Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Fumon-in (Iida)|ja|普門院 (飯田市)}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nagoya)|ja|天神社 (名古屋市中区)}} - Located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|ja|中区 (名古屋市)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Sakura Tenjinja|ja|桜天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Obu)|ja|天神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Nagasago, {{ill|Obu, Aichi|ja|大府市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Nagasago Tenjinja|ja|長草天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Toyokawa)|ja|天神社 (豊川市)}} - Located in Mikamicho, {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|ja|豊川市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Komaki)|ja|天神社 (小牧市)}} - Located in Shimazu, {{ill|Komaki|ja|小牧市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ Tenmantenjinja * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kasukawa)|ja|天神社 (春日井市勝川町)}} - Located in Katsukawacho, {{ill|Kasugai|ja|春日井市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kiyosu)|ja|天神社 (清須市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kiyosu|ja|清須市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mihama)|ja|天神社 (愛知県美浜町)}} - Located in {{ill|Mihama, Aichi|ja|美浜町 (愛知県)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kowa Tenjinja|ja|河和天神社}} * {{ill|Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)|ja|北野天神社 (江南市)}} - Located in Kitanocho, {{ill|Konan, Aichi|ja|江南市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Hokai Tenjinja|ja|宝海天神社}} - Located in Homi Town, {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yoshida Tenjinja|ja|吉田天神社}} - Located in Hirokoji, {{ill|Toyohashi, Aichi|ja|豊橋市}}, Aichi Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Osaka-Fukushima)|ja|天神社 (大阪市福島区)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|ja|福島区}}, Osaka Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Osaka Kita-ku) - Located in Kokubunji, Kita-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Yodogawa Tenjinja|ja|淀川天神社}} * Tenjinja (Osaka Higashisumiyoshi-ku) - Located in Kuwazu, Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Kuwazu Tenjinja|ja|桑津天神社}} * Tenjinja (Toyonaka) - Located in Kamishinden, {{ill|Toyonaka|ja|豊中市}}, Osaka Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kamishinden Tenjinja|ja|上新田天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kawachinagano)|ja|天神社 (河内長野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kadoma)|ja|天神社 (門真市)}} - Located in Tsukidecho, {{ill|Kadoma|ja|門真市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kizugawa)|ja|天神社 (木津川市)}} - Located in Yamashiro, {{ill|Kizugawa|ja|木津川市}}, Kyoto Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nara-Takabatake)|ja|天神社 (奈良市高畑町)}} - Located in Takabatakecho, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yamatotakada)|ja|天神社 (大和高田市)}} - Located in Miwacho, {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Sakurai) - Located in Saimiyama, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja - Located in Kawahara 4-chome, Kusatsu, Shiga Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|ja|天神社 (明石市)}} - Located in {{ill|Akashi|ja|明石市}}, Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|ja|徳島眉山天神社}} - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|ja|眉山町}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|ja|天神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|ja|天神社 (徳島市入田町)}} - Located in Irita, Tokushima.
== Kyushu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Itoshima)|ja|天神社 (糸島市)}} - Located in Kamiaru, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture.
{{Aimai}}

* * {{ill|Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:北野天神社 (江南市)]] - Located in Kitanocho, {{ill|Konan, Aichi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:江南市]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Hokai Tenjinja|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:宝海天神社]] - Located in Homi Town, {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:田原市]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yoshida Tenjinja|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:吉田天神社]] - Located in Hirokoji, {{ill|Toyohashi, Aichi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:豊橋市]], Aichi Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Osaka-Fukushima)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (大阪市福島区)]] - Located in {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島区]], Osaka Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Osaka Kita-ku) - Located in Kokubunji, Kita-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Yodogawa Tenjinja|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:淀川天神社]] * Tenjinja (Osaka Higashisumiyoshi-ku) - Located in Kuwazu, Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Kuwazu Tenjinja|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:桑津天神社]] * Tenjinja (Toyonaka) - Located in Kamishinden, {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kamishinden Tenjinja|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:上新田天神社]] * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kawachinagano)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (河内長野市)]] - Located in Tsukidecho, {{ill|Kadoma|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:門真市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kizugawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (木津川市)]] - Located in Takabatakecho, {{ill|Nara|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:奈良市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yamatotakada)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (大和高田市)]] - Located in Miwacho, {{ill|Yamatotakada|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:大和高田市]], Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Sakurai) - Located in Saimiyama, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja - Located in Kawahara 4-chome, Kusatsu, Shiga Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (明石市)]] - Located in {{ill|Akashi|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:明石市]], Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:徳島眉山天神社]] - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:眉山町]], {{ill|Tokushima|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:徳島市]], Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (徳島市)]] - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天神社 (徳島市入田町)]] - Located in Irita, Tokushima.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== Deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== Deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== Deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. == History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. [[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[Category:q--1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[sn:Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] {{Shrine |Name = Kitano Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg|250px]] |Location = [[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]], [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], Kitanocho Tenjin 8 |LatitudeDegrees = 35 |LatitudeMinutes = 20 |LatitudeSeconds = 7.2 |LongitudeDegrees = 136 |LongitudeMinutes = 52 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.4 |Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]] |ShrineRank = |Founded = [[1654]] ([[Jōō (1652–1655)|Jōō]] 3) |MainHall = |OtherNames = |AnnualFestival = Fude Matsuri (Mid-January, Saturday and Sunday) |Rituals = }}
[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[sn:Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] {{Shrine |Name = Kitano Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg|250px]] |Location = [[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]], [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], Kitanocho Tenjin 8 |LatitudeDegrees = 35 |LatitudeMinutes = 20 |LatitudeSeconds = 7.2 |LongitudeDegrees = 136 |LongitudeMinutes = 52 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.4 |Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]] |ShrineRank = |Founded = [[1654]] ([[Jōō (1652–1655)|Jōō]] 3) |MainHall = |OtherNames = |AnnualFestival = Fude Matsuri (Mid-January, Saturday and Sunday) |Rituals = }}
[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4qq1 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
