[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association
{{Shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine |Name = Kitano Tenjinsha |image = [[File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg|250px]] |Location = [[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]], [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], Kitanocho Tenjin 8 |LatitudeDegrees = 35 |LatitudeMinutes = 20 |LatitudeSeconds = 7.2 |LongitudeDegrees = 136 |LongitudeMinutes = 52 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.4 |Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]] |ShrineRank = |Founded = [[1654]] ([[Jōō (1652–1655)|Jōō]] 3) |MainHall = |OtherNames = |AnnualFestival = Fude Matsuri (Mid-January, Saturday and Sunday) |Rituals = }}
[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[Meitetsu]] [[Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年1月11日 (日) 13:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 北野天神社 | 画像 = [[File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[江南市]]北野町天神8 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 27.4 | 祭神 = [[菅原道真]] | 神体 = | 社格 = | 創建 = [[1654年]]（[[承応]]3年） | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 筆まつり（1月中旬の土日） | 神事 = }}
[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|筆まつりの大筆奉納行列]]
'''北野天神社'''（きたのてんじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[江南市]]北野町天神8にある[[神社]]である。
== 概要== 学業成就、合格祈願、家内安全にご利益があるとされる。無住の神社であるが、1月・2月の日曜日と毎月25日の月次祭には合格祈願などの祈祷が行われる。毎年1月中旬には「筆まつり」が行われ、字の上達を祈願する参拝者や学校合格を祈願する受験生などが訪れる。
== 祭神 == * [[菅原道真]]
== 歴史 == * [[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）に創建された<ref name="江南市観光協会">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 北野天神社] 江南市観光協会</ref>。
== 祭礼 == === 筆まつり === 毎年1月中旬には「筆まつり」が行われる。[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）より開催されている。この祭りのときに境内にある石造りの大筆に願いを書くと、字が上達するといわれている。当初は菅原道真の命日（2月25日）に近い日曜日に開催されていたが、大学受験シーズンにあわせて、1月中旬の土・日曜日に変更されている。受験シーズンに重なることもあり、受験生も多く訪れる。
筆まつりで行われる「大筆奉納行列」は、長さ約4m、重さ約50kgの大筆を、[[厄年]]男性と新入学児が御所車に乗せて神社まで曳き廻すものである。
== 交通アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]] [[江南駅 (愛知県)|江南駅]]より徒歩で約3分<ref name="江南市観光協会"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 北野天神社] 江南市観光協会
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたのてんしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:江南市の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:江南市の建築物]]

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association
{{Shinto-stub}}

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association
{{Shinto-stub}}

== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). == History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]
== History == * Established in [[:en:1654|1654]] (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since [[:en:1964|1964]] (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Inuyama Line]] [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}
== History == * Established in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654}} (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964}} (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line}} [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kitano Tenjinsha |image = File:Konan Kitano-Tenjinsha 2021-07 ac (3).jpg |Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], Kitanocho Tenjin 8 |LatitudeDegrees = 35 |LatitudeMinutes = 20 |LatitudeSeconds = 7.2 |LongitudeDegrees = 136 |LongitudeMinutes = 52 |LongitudeSeconds = 27.4 |Deities = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |ShrineRank = |Founded = {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}} ([[Jōō (1652–1655)|Jōō]] 3) |MainHall = |OtherNames = |AnnualFestival = Fude Matsuri (Mid-January, Saturday and Sunday) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Konan|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.335333|N|136.874278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} }}
[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
== History == * Established in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}} (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
== History == * Established in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}} (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association

[[File:筆まつり (愛知県江南市) - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Fude Matsuri's Giant Brush Procession]]
'''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (北野天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Konan, Aichi|Konan City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == Kitano Tenjinsha is known for providing blessings for academic success, exam passing, and household safety. Although it is an unstaffed shrine, prayer ceremonies for academic success and other requests are conducted on Sundays in January and February, as well as on the 25th of each month. The annual "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) held in mid-January attracts worshippers praying for improved handwriting and students seeking success in school entrance exams.
== deity == * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
== History == * Established in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}} (Jōō 3)<ref name="konan">[https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] Konan City Tourism Association</ref>.
== Festivals == === Fude Matsuri === The "Fude Matsuri" (Brush Festival) is held annually in mid-January. It has been celebrated since {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). Writing wishes on the large stone brush within the shrine grounds is believed to improve handwriting skills. Originally, the festival was held on the Sunday closest to Sugawara no Michizane's death anniversary (February 25), but it was moved to mid-January to align with Japan's university entrance exam season, attracting many students.
A highlight of the festival is the "Giant Brush Procession," in which a brush measuring approximately 4 meters in length and weighing around 50 kg is placed on a ceremonial cart and pulled to the shrine by men in their unlucky age (yakudoshi) and newly enrolled students.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} [[:en:Konan Station (Aichi)|Konan Station]], about a 3-minute walk<ref name="konan"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha (Konan, Aichi)}} * [https://www.konan-kankou.jp/?page_id=4111 Kitano Tenjinsha] - Konan City Tourism Association
